title: Blue Collar Booms and American Mortality: Evidence from the Fracking Revolution
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Figure 2 / main DiD specification. Stata: reghdfe lnearns_gen1_a1499 ddtquartile1 c.medianhhinc1990#i.year ... [aw=pop2000] if balancesample==1, absorb(splay1_year_fe cntyfips2000) vce(cl cntyfips2000). 9 staggered cohorts (2001-2012), 112 treated counties (top-quartile RPI), 407 never-treated control counties (same shale plays, lower RPI). splay1_year_fe = shale_play x year FE (464 categories), replaces standard year FE. Controls are c.X1990#i.year (baseline-x-year interactions) — use feols i(year, X) syntax. cs_controls empty because att_gt cannot handle baseline-x-year interactions. lnearns_gen1_a1499 has 8513 non-NA within balancesample (QWI coverage starts ~2002). Pattern 37 applies. | Outcome selection: Rule (ii) largest N. Mortality data (Table 6) requires restricted-access files not in replication package. Earnings are the primary available outcome in the public replication data. Audit confirmed. | CS-DID dynamic restricted to max_e=5: treatment (ddtquartile1) turns OFF at e~6, so e>=6 captures post-treatment rebound, not causal effect. Unrestricted dynamic gave ATT=0.124 (driven by late horizons with 2-5 cohorts and ATT~0.25); restricted to e<=5 gives ATT=0.018, consistent with TWFE=0.019 and group=0.008.
